title: Futures: A futures contract obligates the buyer to purchase an underlying...
summary: Evaluate whether a futures contract obligates the buyer to purchase the underlying asset.
prompt: A futures contract obligates the buyer to purchase an underlying asset at a specified price at a future date.
solution: This statement is true because a futures contract is a standardized agreement traded on exchanges, which binds the buyer to purchase the asset at the agreed-upon price at the contract's expiration. This characteristic differentiates it from options, which provide the right but not the obligation to buy or sell.
